You are an LLVM maintainer.
Users have reported a case where LLVM failed to optimize a specific program.
You are adding new code or modifying existing code to implement the missing optimization.
Please answer with the code directly. Do not include any additional information in the output.
Please answer with the complete code snippet (including the unmodified part) that replaces the original code. Do not answer with a diff.

The previous test program `@sext_multi_uses` was not successfully optimized.
The previous test program is:
```llvm
define i64 @sext_multi_uses(i64 %a, i1 %b, i64 %x) {
  %c = sext i1 %b to i64
  %d = xor i64 %a, %c
  %e = sub i64 %d, %c
  %f = mul i64 %x, %c
  %r = add i64 %f, %e
  ret i64 %r
}

```
The expected optimized program is:
```llvm
define i64 @sext_multi_uses(i64 %a, i1 %b, i64 %x) {
  %1 = add i64 %x, %a
  %2 = sub i64 0, %1
  %r = select i1 %b, i64 %2, i64 %a
  ret i64 %r
}

```
But the current optimized program is:
```llvm
define i64 @sext_multi_uses(i64 %a, i1 %b, i64 %x) {
  %c = sext i1 %b to i64
  %1 = sub i64 0, %a
  %e = select i1 %b, i64 %1, i64 %a
  %f = mul i64 %c, %x
  %r = add i64 %f, %e
  ret i64 %r
}

```
Please revisit your generated code and adjust it according to the feedback.

Please answer with the code directly. Do not include any additional information in the output.
Please answer with the complete code snippet (including the unmodified part) that replaces the original code. Do not answer with a diff.
Please make sure the answer includes the prefix:
```cpp

      (Op0->hasOneUse() || Op1->hasOneUse())) {
    Value *And = Builder.CreateAnd(X, Y, "mulbool");
    return CastInst::Create(Instruction::SExt, And, Ty);
  }
```
and the suffix:
```cpp
    // (ashr i32 X, 31) * C --> (X < 0) ? -C : 0
    const APInt *C;
    if (match(Op0, m_OneUse(m_AShr(m_Value(X), m_APInt(C)))) &&
        *C == C->getBitWidth() - 1) {

```

define i64 @sext_diff_i1_xor_sub_1(i64 %a, i1 %b, i1 %c) {
; CHECK-LABEL: define i64 @sext_diff_i1_xor_sub_1(
; CHECK-SAME: i64 [[A:%.*]], i1 [[B:%.*]], i1 [[C:%.*]]) {
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[D:%.*]] = sext i1 [[B]] to i64
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[E_NEG:%.*]] = zext i1 [[C]] to i64
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[R:%.*]] = add nsw i64 [[E_NEG]], [[D]]
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i64 [[R]]
  %d = sext i1 %b to i64
  %e = sext i1 %c to i64
  %f = xor i64 %d, %a
  %r = sub i64 %d, %e
  ret i64 %r
}

define i64 @sext_multi_uses(i64 %a, i1 %b, i64 %x) {
  %c = sext i1 %b to i64
  %d = xor i64 %a, %c
  %e = sub i64 %d, %c
  %f = mul i64 %x, %c
  %r = add i64 %f, %e
  ret i64 %r
}
